name: Creole Garlic Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Add the garlic cloves to the beef broth in a saucepan; cover and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the garlic is soft.
2. Remove the garlic cloves using a slotted spoon and set them aside. Discard the garlic cloves.
3. Add water and sherry to the broth and heat until it reaches serving temperature.
4. Toast bread slices on one side under the broiler until lightly browned; remove from the oven and spread the untoasted side with softened butter.
5. Mash the reserved garlic cloves using a fork or mortar and pestle, then spread over the toasted bread slices.
6. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese evenly over the mashed garlic and bread.
7. Broil the toast under the broiler for about 30 seconds until it is lightly browned and bubbly.
8. Divide the hot soup among four soup bowls.
9. Place a piece of toasted bread in each bowl.
10. Ladle the remaining soup into each bowl and serve.
